summary: This lecture from Riyadul Saliheen (Gardens of the Righteous) by Al-Hafiz Al-Nawawi discusses a hadith narrated by Usama ibn Zaid about the Prophet Muhammad's response to the death of his granddaughter's child. The speaker explains how the Prophet sent a message of condolence with words of patience and acceptance of Allah's decree, emphasizing that the most important thing during such trials is to hear and believe in these words rather than physical presence. The hadith illustrates the Prophet's compassion and tears upon holding the dying infant, demonstrating that showing emotion is not contrary to patience and submission to Allah's will.
suggested_title: Riyadul Saliheen: The Prophet's Response to the Death of a Child - Lessons on Patience and Divine Decree
hadith_chapter: Chapter on Patience and Acceptance of Divine Decree
catchy_title: When the Prophet's Tears Met Allah's Will
